HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

5
6#include "Dipole.h"
7#include <iostream>
8
9// ---------------------------
10// Constructors
11// ---------------------------
12Dipole::Dipole(rvec location, cvec polarization, dcmplx vacuumWavelength,
13 dcmplx mu, GreenF* fHom)
14 : p(polarization),
15 r0(location),
16 omega(2 * PI * CVAC / vacuumWavelength),
17 grnFun(fHom),
18 muMedium(mu) { grnFunLayered = nullptr; grnFunLayeredFields = nullptr;}
19
20Dipole::Dipole(rvec location, cvec polarization, dcmplx vacuumWavelength,
21 GreenF* fHom, dcmplx mu, std::vector<Grid> tabGrids,
22 GreenF* fLayered, LayeredMediaUtils* utils)
23 : p(polarization),
24 r0(location),
25 omega(2 * PI * CVAC / vacuumWavelength),
26 grnFun(fHom),
27 muMedium(mu),
28 tabulationGrids(tabGrids),
29 grnFunLayered(fLayered),
30 layeredUtils(utils) { grnFunLayeredFields = nullptr; }
31
32// ---------------------------
33// Pointwise fields (homogeneous)
34// ---------------------------
35cvec Dipole::EvaluateE(rvec r) {
36 return omega * omega * MU0 * muMedium * dot(grnFun->EvaluateDyadic(r, r0), p);
37}
38
39cvec Dipole::EvaluateH(rvec r) {
40 // Minus because Gradient() is with respect to second parameter
41 return I * omega * cross(grnFun->Gradient(r, r0), p);
42}
43
44// ---------------------------
45// Pointwise fields (layered)
46// ---------------------------
47std::vector<cvec> Dipole::EvaluateLayeredEandH(rvec r)
48{
49 // Start with zero fields
50 cvec Einc(dcmplx(0.0, 0.0), dcmplx(0.0, 0.0), dcmplx(0.0, 0.0));
51 cvec Hinc(dcmplx(0.0, 0.0), dcmplx(0.0, 0.0), dcmplx(0.0, 0.0));
52
53 // Homogeneous contribution added only if r and r0 reside in the same layer
54 int obsLayerIndex = this->layeredUtils->GetLayerIndex(r);
55 int incLayerIndex = this->layeredUtils->GetLayerIndex(r0);
56 if (obsLayerIndex == incLayerIndex) {
57 Einc += this->EvaluateE(r);
58 Hinc += this->EvaluateH(r);
59 }
60
61 // Layered Green's function contributions
62 if (!grnFunLayeredFields) {
63 throw std::runtime_error("Green function not set on this Dipole.");
64 }
65 // Downcast to GreenFLayered3D to access specific methods
66 auto* grnPtr = dynamic_cast<GreenFLayered3D*>(grnFunLayeredFields);
67 if (!grnPtr) {
68 throw std::runtime_error("Green function pointer is not a GreenFLayered3D.");
69 }
70
71 // Set dyadic type to "normal" for E field contribution
72 Einc += omega * omega * MU0 * muMedium * dot(grnPtr->EvaluateDyadic(r, r0, "normal"), p);
73
74 // Set dyadic type to "curl" for H field contribution
75 Hinc += I * omega * dot(grnPtr->EvaluateDyadic(r, r0, "curl"), p);
76
77 return {Einc, Hinc};
78}
79
80// ---------------------------
81// RWG integrations (homogeneous and layered)
82// ---------------------------
83dcmplx Dipole::IntegrateE(RWGFun* f) {
84 return omega * omega * MU0 * muMedium * dot(p, grnFun->IntegrateDelta(r0, f));
85}
86
87dcmplx Dipole::IntegrateH(RWGFun* f) {
88 return I * omega * dot(p, grnFun->IntegrateKappa(r0, f));
89}
90
91// Combine direct (homogeneous) and reflected/transmitted (layered) parts.
92std::vector<dcmplx> Dipole::IntegrateLayeredEandH(RWGFun* f) {
93 dcmplx Einc = omega * omega * MU0 * muMedium *
94 ( dot( p, grnFun->IntegrateDelta( r0, f ) ) +
95 dot( p, grnFunLayered->IntegrateDelta( r0, f ) ) );
96 dcmplx Hinc = I * omega *
97 ( dot( p, grnFun->IntegrateKappa( r0, f ) ) +
98 dot( p, grnFunLayered->IntegrateKappa( r0, f ) ) );
99 return {Einc, Hinc};
100}
101
102// ---------------------------
103// Accessors & configuration
104// ---------------------------
105rvec Dipole::Location() { return r0; }
106
107cvec Dipole::Polarization() { return p; }
108
109// Set Green's function for layered media
110void Dipole::setGrnFunLayered(GreenF* fGrnFields, std::vector<Grid> tabGrids) {
111 grnFunLayeredFields = fGrnFields;
112 tabulationGridsFields = tabGrids;
113}
Dipole.h
Incident field generator for electric dipole illumination in homogeneous or layered media.

dcmplx muMedium
Relative magnetic permeability of the background medium.
